title: ranunculus auricomus subsp. glabratus
intro: Ranunculus auricomus subsp. glabratus, also known as the hairy lesser celandine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Ranunculus auricomus subsp. glabratus can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is sometimes used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
